conditions.message: The only repetition is the established top-level/internal scaffold template mirror and mirrored user-facing route guidance; the new contract test avoids copy-pasted assertions by using shared needle slices and loops.

templates/ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl and internal/scaffold/templates/ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl receive the same Role Split section to keep the shipped template and embedded scaffold template aligned, matching the repo's existing mirror pattern.

templates/CLAUDE.md.orchestrator.tmpl and internal/scaffold/templates/CLAUDE.md.orchestrator.tmpl receive the same short route-level instruction because either generated surface must stand alone for Claude routing.
TestOrchestratorRouteTemplatesCarryRoleSplit defines orchestratorNeedles, routeNeedles, and skillNeedles once, then loops across mirrored paths rather than duplicating nearly identical test bodies.

conditions.message: The staged guidance reduces reward-hacking risk by explicitly forbidding orchestrator self-implementation/self-verification and by requiring worker evidence and provenance; the test checks concrete template text rather than superficial completion labels.
evidence: The Role Split text states that the orchestrator does not implement, edit docs, run verifier feature-condition cells, or execute commit-gate payload work itself when workers are available.
The skill template states that workers are the implementers and checkers and instructs the orchestrator to preserve their actual evidence and provenance.

The CLAUDE route template limits the session to triage, decision capture, routing, review, gate operation, evidence recording, Agent Mail coordination, and pane wakes, preventing pane wake or coordination activity from being treated as verifier evidence.
TestOrchestratorRouteTemplatesCarryRoleSplit asserts these exact role-split phrases across ORCHESTRATOR, CLAUDE route, and skill templates.
